what_changed: connection-pool-monitor detected active connection count crossing 95% saturation threshold; metric emitted to alerting-pipeline
connection-pool-monitor crossed the 95% saturation threshold (190/200 connections) and delegated classification to alerting-pipeline; threshold breach, not direct system failure
alerting-pipeline translated raw saturation metric into Severity-1 incident classification using escalation-rule-engine; pagerduty-webhook triggered
on-call-engineer decided to increase connection pool limit from 200 to 400 as immediate mitigation; change deployed via config-management-service
pagerduty-webhook delivery lagged 75 seconds from initial metric emission due to retry queue congestion under flash sale load
uncertainty_note: The 75-second lag is noted in the incident timeline but not explained in the alert metadata. It is unclear whether the congestion was caused by flash sale load or an unrelated queue buildup at the time.

what_changed: prometheus-alertmanager identified connection pool saturation (190/200) and routed alert to alerting-pipeline
uncertainty_note: The incident report is ambiguous about which component performed the initial detection. Analyst-b reads this as Prometheus alerting rather than a direct pool monitor threshold — the source span does not name either system explicitly.

what_changed: prometheus-alertmanager crossed saturation threshold and escalated to alerting-pipeline; alert routing constitutes a regime change from monitoring to incident response
on-call-engineer received Severity-1 page from pagerduty-webhook; the engineer's receipt of the alert, not the pipeline's routing decision, is the meaningful event in this span
uncertainty_note: Analyst-b reads this span as documenting the engineer's receipt of the page. Analyst-a reads the same span as documenting the pipeline's classification act. The source span is compatible with both readings.

what_changed: on-call-engineer decided to increase connection pool limit from 200 to 400 as immediate mitigation; change deployed via config-management-service
flash-sale-event generated 3x baseline request volume; connection-pool demand exceeded provisioned capacity as a result
uncertainty_note: The flash sale load pattern is present in the incident timeline but analyst-a did not extract it as a separate span. Analyst-b treats it as the causal precondition that made the saturation event possible.
